one_time_description: Impact Management and Benefit Agreement (IMBA) and Economic and Community Development Agreement (ECDA) revenue of $7,110,812 — significantly above prior year $848,418; also a pending Timber Surrender Specific Claim Settlement of $7,067,507 not yet received
economic_notes: GBEs include aqam Trading LP (gas station and convenience store), aqam Resources LP (forestry, mineral/gravel extraction, renewable energy), aqam Community Enterprises LP. Pending Timber Surrender Specific Claim Settlement of $7,067,507 not yet received as at March 31, 2023. IMBA/ECDA reserves grew significantly to $22.3M.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2023 (not 2024 as template default). AQAM is a Ktunaxa Nation community in BC. Five GBEs/GBPs accounted for on modified equity basis (not consolidated). No long-term debt on balance sheet. Large IMBA/ECDA revenue ($7.1M vs $848K prior year) is a significant one-time/variable item. A Timber Surrender Specific Claim Settlement of $7,067,507 is pending but not yet received or recorded. Segmented schedule (Schedule 2) referenced but not fully provided in text — expense allocations estimated from Schedule 1 line items. expenses_infrastructure includes Maintenance of Facilities ($1,270,375) and Water System ($78,928). expenses_education includes Education ($472,762) and Elementary School ($1,419,867). Trust funds of $102,725 administered by aqam are excluded from financial statements.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
